title: A tenant-created ring revocation is executed as estate-wide Transit key deletion

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: Any authenticated principal can submit a typed resource create.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Every live Ring(n) intent is treated as authority to darken all TrustRings with that number; tenant, token, owner, role, and intent status are not bound.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: The controller AppRole enables deletion and deletes the base Transit key plus every prefix-matching per-tenant derivative.

source: An authenticated low-privilege tenant can create RevocationIntent{target: Ring(n)}; admission stamps its tenant but applies no per-kind or ring authority rule.
closest_control: RevocationIntent validation only bounds n to 1..=3; rings.rs checks target equality and non-termination but never binds metadata.tenant, token_ref, owner_refs, role, or an operator approval to the shared ring.
sink_broken_control: TrustRingController invokes TransitAdmin::disable_key_family, which authenticates as the controller AppRole and performs irreversible key-family deletion.
impact: A tenant token can make every envelope in a shared ring permanently unreadable and halt every workload in that ring, causing cross-tenant destructive denial of service.
plausibility: The full static chain is present, and crates/aog-controller/tests/live_ring.rs:337-389 demonstrates that a Ring(3) intent removes the live Transit key, breaks unseal, and halts the workload.

candidate_local_validation.method: Static end-to-end trace plus checked-in live OpenBao integration evidence
candidate_local_validation.evidence: AdmissionPolicy assigns RevocationIntent empty policy facts; rings.rs fans the intent into disable_key_family; the live test asserts key_version becomes None and unseal fails.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: No non-test binary in this snapshot instantiates TrustRingController; deployment reachability depends on the external controller-manager composition.

candidate_local_attack_path.attacker: Any holder of a valid tenant WSF token
candidate_local_attack_path.path: authenticated create -> unrestricted admission -> ring informer -> system-principal controller -> OpenBao AppRole -> Transit config/delete
candidate_local_attack_path.outcome: Cross-tenant key destruction and workload halt
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: Requires only ordinary authentication; the privileged effect is global and destructive. Live-system test proves the sink when the controller is composed.

title: Tenant-controlled PolicyBundle desired state is re-signed and published as trusted estate policy

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: A tenant principal is blocked only when the current object has a different nonempty tenant; system/global tenant=None objects remain mutable and tenant is preserved as None.

affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation
affected_locations.detail: The controller trusts the admitted PolicyBundle spec and signs/publishes it without checking tenant, token_ref, owner, or authorizing role.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: The control-plane signer turns the supplied mode/rules/version into offline-verifiable authority.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: The signed bundle is written to a global, non-tenant-namespaced OpenBao poll path.

source: An authenticated tenant updates a known system/global PolicyBundle (metadata.tenant=None) or creates a tenant-stamped bundle that the global controller processes.
closest_control: Admission validates structure and classification/compliance facts, but PolicyBundle has no per-kind authorization facts; the controller does not independently bind signer use or publication scope to a trusted operator/tenant.
sink_broken_control: sign_bundle uses the control-plane ML-DSA signer and OpenBaoBundleStore publishes the resulting artifact on the estate channel.
impact: A tenant can cause attacker-selected Shadow/Report policy or routing rules to become a trusted signed artifact, potentially weakening enforcement for other tenants and all subscribed gateways/nodes.
plausibility: Direct source-to-signer-to-OpenBao trace; the global update precondition is independently present in admission.rs and live_bundle.rs proves publication/edge verification behavior.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: PolicyBundle is absent from AdmissionPolicy authority facts; global update accepts tenant principal when current tenant is None; bundles.rs signs spec fields verbatim and store path has no tenant component.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: No non-test binary in this snapshot instantiates PolicyBundleController or EdgeBundleCache; the exact production poll/subscription name is external to this crate.

candidate_local_attack_path.path: admission global-object gap -> PolicyBundleController -> sign_bundle -> OpenBaoBundleStore -> verifying edge
candidate_local_attack_path.outcome: Attacker-selected policy is endorsed by the control-plane key and distributed
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: Crosses a signing-authority boundary and can become estate-wide; deployment composition remains a precondition.

title: The signed active-grant set collapses all tenant grants into one global authorization set

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: GrantEntry carries only tool and systems; active_grants lists every tenant and discards tenant, owner, requires_approval, credential_ref, and status.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: Edge authorization checks only tool and optional system against the global signed set.

affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation
affected_locations.detail: Any ToolGrant change causes the union to be signed and published.

source: Authenticated tenants can create tenant-stamped ToolGrant objects; controller list returns all tenants' grants.
closest_control: Per-object tenant stamping protects mutations, but no tenant/mission/subject identity survives the projection into GrantEntry and no caller context is accepted by EdgeGrantCache::allows or allows_system.
sink_broken_control: A proxy presented with the set authorizes a tool for every caller/edge if any tenant granted that tool.
impact: One tenant's grant can authorize tool execution and system access for other tenants; requires_approval and credential binding are also erased before the decision.
plausibility: Deterministic type/data-flow proof. Existing tests intentionally construct a single global store/cache and assert estate-wide propagation, but contain no multi-tenant negative case.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: GrantEntry has no tenant field; active_grants consumes grant.metadata but copies only spec.tool/systems; EdgeGrantCache methods accept no tenant or mission argument.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Repository search found EdgeGrantCache and ToolGrantController only in this crate and its tests, with no non-test toolproxy/controller-manager integration.

candidate_local_attack_path.attacker: Tenant able to create its own ToolGrant
candidate_local_attack_path.path: global list -> lossy GrantEntry projection -> signed set -> edge allows(tool/system)
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: Would be high when connected to a credential-minting tool proxy; current runtime reachability is not established in this snapshot.

title: Mission system-scope reductions do not update existing derived ToolGrants

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Desired grants are keyed by name; an existing name suppresses create, and no update compares or converges tool/systems/spec.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: The stale grant is published, and empty systems means unrestricted access at the edge.

source: An authorized mission owner changes allowed_systems from empty/unrestricted to a restricted set, or otherwise narrows systems without changing the tool name.
closest_control: The controller prunes removed grant names, but it does not compare the existing ToolGrant spec with the desired spec and therefore does not propagate same-name scope changes.
sink_broken_control: The old ToolGrant remains active and ToolGrantController signs its stale broader systems list.
impact: A withdrawn system authorization remains usable after the mission contract has narrowed it, defeating revocation and mission scope enforcement.
plausibility: Deterministic state transition: gname remains in existing_names and desired, so neither create/update nor delete executes. Tests cover removed tools but not allowed_systems updates.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: mission.rs:201 skips every existing gname; lines 223-228 delete only names absent from desired. No ToolGrant update call exists in the file.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: The downstream ToolGrantController/EdgeGrantCache path has no non-test composition in this snapshot.

candidate_local_attack_path.attacker: Mission agent or caller retaining access to a previously permitted system
candidate_local_attack_path.path: reconcile skips existing grant -> stale empty/broader systems published -> allows_system continues to allow
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: Security impact is direct in the library semantics; deployed reachability is unproven.

title: Mission grant ownership is matched by namesake rather than immutable owner UID and tenant

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: owned_grants accepts any OwnerRef with matching kind/name and ignores UID and grant tenant.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: The namesake stale grant suppresses creation of a correctly owned current grant.

affected_locations.label: supporting_control
affected_locations.detail: OwnerRef.uid is explicitly the identity boundary that distinguishes a recreated namesake.

source: A MissionContract is deleted and recreated with the same name while a prior owned ToolGrant remains, or an orphan is observed before GC convergence.
closest_control: The object model provides immutable OwnerRef.uid, but owned_grants checks only kind and name; no tenant equality is checked either.
sink_broken_control: A stale broader ToolGrant can be treated as current, avoid replacement, and remain in the signed active-grant set.
impact: A recreated mission can inherit authorization from a prior owner identity or tenant, preserving withdrawn tool/system authority across deletion/recreation.
plausibility: Exact mismatch with the documented OwnerRef invariant; controller create convergence and existing-name suppression make the stale object security-relevant before GC closes the orphan.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: OwnerRef docs require UID matching; mission owned_grants omits it, then existing_names prevents replacement. GC is asynchronous and therefore not an equivalent pre-check.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: No runtime harness was executed for the delete/recreate race, and the downstream grant publisher is not composed by a non-test binary.

candidate_local_attack_path.attacker: Tenant that can recreate a mission namesake or race GC
candidate_local_attack_path.path: orphaned grant retained -> name-only owner match -> create skipped -> stale grant remains active
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: Requires lifecycle timing and deployed O5/O6 composition; exact root control is present.

title: Mission call ceilings trust caller-mutable, non-reserved status counters

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: mission_allows authorizes from a supplied calls_used snapshot and performs no reservation or increment.

affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation
affected_locations.detail: Controller preserves existing calls_used and only changes phase; it never meters calls.

affected_locations.label: supporting_control
affected_locations.detail: Ordinary updates can replace status, and stamp_update does not preserve or restrict controller-owned status fields.

source: A tenant can submit a lower calls_used status for its mission, or concurrent callers can evaluate the same below-ceiling snapshot.
closest_control: CAS protects each object update, but mission_allows is a pure check detached from an atomic counter update and admission has no status subresource/ownership rule.
sink_broken_control: Each racing or reset caller receives Allow and can proceed to the privileged tool action beyond call_ceiling.
impact: Mission tool-call blast-radius limits can be reset or overspent, allowing more privileged actions than the declared contract.
plausibility: No code in mission.rs increments calls_used; repository search finds only reads/controller preservation for this estate status. The separately implemented aog-toolproxy mission tally is not wired to this estate contract.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: mission_allows reads status.calls_used once; set_status copies it unchanged; admission stamp_update preserves identity metadata but not status. Two checks at N-1 can both allow.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Repository search found no non-test caller of controller::mission_allows; actual aog-toolproxy uses a separate in-memory Mission type.

candidate_local_attack_path.attacker: Mission caller able to issue concurrent actions or update its own estate object
candidate_local_attack_path.path: non-atomic snapshot check -> Allow -> tool action without shared reservation
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: Concrete invariant failure but current production call-site reachability is absent.

title: Grant-set anti-rollback state is lost on edge restart

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: The applied version exists only in process memory and new() always starts with None.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: A valid old set is accepted when no in-memory current version exists and immediately authorizes its revoked tools.

source: A replayable distribution channel or removable-media input offers an older correctly signed grant set after a proxy restart.
closest_control: accept rejects offered<=applied only while applied survives in memory; no durable monotonic floor, signed epoch, expiry, or external freshness anchor is consulted.
sink_broken_control: The replayed set becomes applied and EdgeGrantCache authorizes tools removed by a newer pre-restart set.
impact: Restart can resurrect revoked privileged tool grants despite the documented anti-rollback guarantee.
plausibility: Deterministic: a new EdgeGrantCache accepts any valid version, including v1 after a prior process had accepted v2. The existing stale replay test does not simulate restart.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: new sets applied=None; accept checks staleness only under Some(current). No persistence API exists.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: No non-test EdgeGrantCache integrator or distribution-store implementation is present in the snapshot.

candidate_local_attack_path.attacker: Channel replayer with any previously signed grant artifact
candidate_local_attack_path.entrypoint: Proxy restart followed by set fetch
candidate_local_attack_path.path: volatile version floor cleared -> signature passes -> stale check skipped -> old grants applied
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: No signing-key compromise required; deployed reachability is unproven.

title: Policy-bundle anti-rollback state is lost on edge restart

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Per-name applied versions are held only in an in-memory HashMap initialized empty.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: A correctly signed old bundle is accepted after restart because no current entry exists.

affected_locations.label: supporting_source
affected_locations.detail: KV-v2 retraction soft-deletes only the latest version and does not establish a durable edge rollback floor.

source: A replayable OpenBao/removable-media channel offers an older correctly signed policy bundle after an edge restart.
closest_control: Version comparison is process-local; no persisted version floor, signed epoch/expiry, monotonic counter, or current-generation binding survives restart.
sink_broken_control: The old bundle becomes the applied policy for its signed name, potentially restoring Shadow/Report mode or withdrawn allow rules.
impact: A restart permits validly signed policy downgrade and can silently weaken enforcement until a newer bundle is observed.
plausibility: Deterministic constructor/state proof; current tests reject v1 after v2 in one process but never recreate the cache between versions.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: new initializes applied empty; accept checks version only if the map already contains bundle.name. KV retract is soft delete and does not persist edge state.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: No non-test EdgeBundleCache integrator is present; production edge persistence and channel replay resistance may exist outside this repository snapshot.

candidate_local_attack_path.attacker: Channel replayer or compromised/lagging OpenBao able to serve an old signed artifact
candidate_local_attack_path.path: rollback floor cleared -> signature verifies -> stale branch absent -> old policy applied
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: No signing key needed; requires restart and a replayable artifact source.
